R0 Score Distribution (click bar to drill down)

AIS Distribution (click bar to drill down)

▦ AIS x R0 Cross-Tab

Impression Share % (within selected BU) (click cell to drill down)

The hottest cell is AIS 30–35% x R0 50–60% at 12.94% of total impressions. There's a clear diagonal pattern — moderate-AIS stores tend to have moderate R0. The bottom-left (high AIS + low R0) is where user experience risk is highest, but it's only ~2.30% of impressions. Use the BU filter above to drill into each BU.
★ Store Segments

Segment — Store Count

Segment — Impression Share

☰ Store-Level Insights
⌛ Query-Level AIS

We have excluded query having <1000 impressions.

Query AIS distribution (click bar for top 30 queries, CSV)

⚙ Appendix: Store AIS vs Query AIS — Why the Distributions Differ ▶
The Store-Level AIS chart shows that the >40% bucket holds only ~5.75% of total impressions, while the Query-Level AIS chart shows the same bucket at ~28.54% — the highest. This is expected and correct. Below is a summary of why.

How the two metrics differ

Store-Level AIS chart Query-Level AIS chart
Unit 1 store 1 (store, query) row
AIS value Weighted average across all queries in the store AIS for that specific query in that store
Averaging effect High & low query AIS values average out → fewer extreme buckets Each query keeps its own AIS → extremes are visible

Where do >40% query AIS impressions come from? (by Store AIS bucket)

Over 70% of >40% query-AIS impressions sit in stores whose overall AIS is only 30–40%.

Store AIS bucket Rows Impressions % of >40% query imp
5–15%551,98,5780.09%
15–25%2,3521,27,89,0805.52%
25–30%3,1011,83,08,5087.90%
30–35%9,8328,37,58,13136.13%
35–40%11,1957,96,65,66734.37%
>40%5,9843,70,82,47016.00%

Top 10 queries driving the >40% query AIS bucket

High-volume head queries with >40% AIS, often sitting in stores whose overall AIS is 30–35%.

# Query All impressions
1shoes for men49,82,824
2watch for men21,69,545
3watch for women19,86,088
4earbuds17,62,692
5t shirt for men17,03,748
6shoes14,68,546
7jeans for women12,87,123
8trolley bag12,10,518
9sandals for women12,06,660
10shoes for women10,58,325
Example: "shoes for men" (49.8L impressions, query AIS = 42.01%) lives in store osp/cil (Men's Footwear) whose store-level AIS is 30–35%. In the store chart, all of osp/cil's impressions count in the 30–35% bucket. In the query chart, this query's 49.8L impressions count in the >40% bucket. This pattern repeats across all high-volume head queries — the store average dilutes individual query extremes, but the query-level view preserves them.
Generated from relevance_data_for_04_march.csv · Filters: fold ≤ 12, 7 BUs, top 99% impression stores
